name: ETLAS-2 observed no mental well-being difference with daily tadalafil in CSVD
statement: In older CSVD patients, ETLAS-2 reported no difference in mental well-being after 3 months of tadalafil 20 mg daily versus placebo, while adverse events were more frequent and adherence was lower with tadalafil.
population_or_context: Older CSVD patients with prior stroke or transient ischemic attack.
quantitative_note: Abstract states no differences were observed in cognition, mental well-being, or blood pressure; adverse events occurred in 76% with tadalafil versus 36% with placebo.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports no mental well-being difference and adverse-event imbalance.

caveats: Mental well-being was a secondary outcome.
Tadalafil dose was 20 mg daily, higher than common low-dose daily ED/BPH regimens.
population_profile: CSVD patients with previous stroke or transient ischemic attack; mean age about 68.
endpoint_measure: Mental well-being, reported in the source as assessed alongside depression, fatigue, and WHO-5 well-being measures.
effect_estimate: No mental well-being difference observed; adverse events 76% tadalafil versus 36% placebo.
safety_findings: Higher adverse-event frequency and lower adherence with tadalafil.
directness_to_target_population: Low directness because participants were older CSVD patients, not mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Secondary outcome in a feasibility-primary phase II trial; disease population and high daily dose limit transfer.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; population, dose, and disease-context limits prevent broad transfer to healthy well-being.
